description: AksharaMD downstream LLM accuracy benchmark corpus — v0.3.3
reproducibility_notice: Raw document files are NOT committed to this repository (size and third-party licensing). The Q&A pairs (eval_corpus_qa.yaml) and all result tables (benchmarks/results/README.md and benchmarks/LLM_QA_BENCHMARK.md) are committed. See the 'corpus' section below for per-format source and public availability.

full_benchmark.note: Numbers reported in benchmarks/results/README.md and benchmarks/LLM_QA_BENCHMARK.md
full_benchmark.corpus_location: Local only — not committed. Assembled from the public sources listed below plus synthetic files generated for this benchmark.

judges.note: AksharaMD vs MarkItDown, 12 documents per format
AksharaMD vs MarkItDown, ~8 documents per format

validation_subset.note: The 100-document subset committed to this repo as benchmarks/eval_corpus_qa.yaml
validation_subset.path_format: Absolute local paths (e.g. C:\Users\...\benchmark_corpus_v5\...). Update paths to match your local corpus location before running.
validation_subset.what_is_committed: Q&A pairs only — questions and expected answers. Raw document files are not included.
validation_subset.what_you_can_run: Token-count benchmark (--no-llm) requires only the Q&A YAML and the documents themselves. Full accuracy benchmark requires documents plus an ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY, or OPENAI_API_KEY.

pdf.selection: Spanning simple single-column, multi-column layout, table-heavy, and math-heavy papers
pdf.public_availability: PUBLICLY RE-DOWNLOADABLE. Filenames follow the arXiv paper-ID pattern (e.g. 2606.28277v1.pdf). Download from https://arxiv.org/pdf/<paper-id>.

docx.selection: Spanning short single-section through multi-chapter documents with embedded tables
docx.public_availability: SYNTHETIC — not publicly reproducible. Generated for this benchmark; cannot be downloaded from a public source.

pptx.selection: Spanning minimal-text through data-heavy slide decks
pptx.public_availability: SYNTHETIC — not publicly reproducible. Generated for this benchmark; cannot be downloaded from a public source.

xlsx.selection: Spanning single-sheet through multi-sheet workbooks with varying cell density
xlsx.public_availability: SYNTHETIC — not publicly reproducible. Generated for this benchmark; cannot be downloaded from a public source.

html.selection: Long-form encyclopedia articles with heavy navigation markup to stress boilerplate removal
html.public_availability: PUBLICLY RE-DOWNLOADABLE. Filenames follow the article slug pattern (e.g. black_hole.html). Download from https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/<article-name>.

epub.selection: Public domain books spanning fiction, non-fiction, and technical works
epub.public_availability: PUBLICLY RE-DOWNLOADABLE. Filenames follow the Gutenberg book-number pattern (e.g. gutenberg_11.epub). Download from https://www.gutenberg.org/ebooks/<book-id>.

txt.selection: Plain-text versions of public domain books; same titles as EPUB set where available
txt.public_availability: PUBLICLY RE-DOWNLOADABLE. Filenames follow the Gutenberg book-number pattern (e.g. gutenberg_11.txt). Download from https://www.gutenberg.org/ebooks/<book-id>.

eml.selection: Spanning plain-text, HTML-body, and multi-part messages with attachments
eml.public_availability: SYNTHETIC — not publicly reproducible. Generated for this benchmark; cannot be downloaded from a public source.

ipynb.selection: Notebooks with varying ratios of code cells to markdown cells
ipynb.public_availability: SYNTHETIC — not publicly reproducible. Modelled on public notebook patterns but the specific files used were generated for this benchmark.

csv.selection: Files with row counts from dozens to thousands; varying column complexity
csv.public_availability: SYNTHETIC — not publicly reproducible. Generated for this benchmark; cannot be downloaded from a public source.

json.selection: Flat through deeply nested structures; varying key and value verbosity
json.public_availability: SYNTHETIC — not publicly reproducible. Generated for this benchmark; cannot be downloaded from a public source.

xml.selection: Small, medium, and large files; varying attribute and element density
xml.public_availability: SYNTHETIC — not publicly reproducible. Generated for this benchmark; cannot be downloaded from a public source.

qa_generation.question_type: Factual — specific, verifiable facts: names, numbers, dates, identifiers

evaluation_protocol.context: First 6000 characters of each tool's output (~4000 tokens)
evaluation_protocol.scoring: 0–10 per answer; 10 = fully correct, 0 = wrong or irrelevant

reproduce.update_paths: Edit benchmarks/eval_corpus_qa.yaml — replace the 'path:' values with paths to your local copies of the documents
reproduce.token_only: python -m benchmarks.llm_qa_eval --qa benchmarks/eval_corpus_qa.yaml --tools aksharamd markitdown llamaparse pymupdf4llm docling --no-llm
reproduce.full_claude: python -m benchmarks.llm_qa_eval --qa benchmarks/eval_corpus_qa.yaml --tools aksharamd markitdown llamaparse pymupdf4llm docling --llms claude
reproduce.full_gemini: python -m benchmarks.llm_qa_eval --qa benchmarks/eval_corpus_qa.yaml --tools aksharamd markitdown --llms gemini
reproduce.full_openai: python -m benchmarks.llm_qa_eval --qa benchmarks/eval_corpus_qa.yaml --tools aksharamd markitdown --llms openai
reproduce.note: Running against the committed 100-document subset will reproduce the validation-subset scores, not the full 996-document benchmark scores reported in the results tables. To reproduce the full benchmark, assemble the complete corpus (see 'corpus' section above for sources) and regenerate eval_corpus_qa.yaml.
